detailed_analysis: src/agents_sync/cursor_io.py is a Layer-3 interface adapter (per architecture.md §4) that implements the CustomizationTypeIO triple (parse/render/extract_pair_id) for Cursor's five customization types: agents, skills, rules, slash commands, and MCP servers. The module is healthy: 399 lines but structured as a flat catalog of short, single-responsibility functions, none exceeding ~45 lines and most under 25. It delegates all shared mechanics (frontmatter splitting, markdown rendering, MCP dialect parse/render, rules parse/render) to dedicated helper modules, keeping each Cursor function thin. The module correctly threads the configured secret_policy through both MCP egress boundaries (parse_cursor_mcp_server_json, render_cursor_mcp_server_json), satisfying NFR-15 / goal 6. It references only the concrete tool name 'cursor' (legitimate for an adapter; the architecture forbids concrete tool names only in the sync engine, not in adapters). The Dependency Rule is upheld: it imports only inward (canonical, artifact_names, and sibling Layer-3 IO helpers) and no Layer-2/Layer-4 modules. No CRITICAL or MAJOR anti-patterns found. The only findings are minor: the parse functions accept a positional prior_canonical plus three keyword-only Path arguments and immediately mutate a canonical dict (a shared signature shape across four parse functions, near the W4/data-clump boundary but mandated by the CustomizationTypeIO port contract, so not a true defect); some magic string literals in the MCP transport-default helper; and a near-duplicate parse/render body shape between the agent and skill paths that stays just below the Rule-of-Three threshold given their differing field sets. The 399-line module length exceeds the project's 300-line soft cap (NFR-14 / guideline §3), the single notable structural observation.

findings.variant: Module length over project soft cap
findings.problem: The module exceeds the project's 300-line file soft cap (AGENTS.md §3 / NFR-14). It does NOT meet the God Module threshold (>500 lines, >3 unrelated responsibility clusters): all functions serve a single cohesive responsibility — Cursor native-format <-> canonical translation. The five customization-type sections (agents/skills/rules/commands/MCP) are facets of one concern, not divergent responsibilities. The overage is driven by the breadth of Cursor's five supported customization types, each needing a parse/render/extract triple. Splitting into per-type submodules is the conventional remedy, but cohesion is high; this is a soft-cap note, not structural decay.

The transport key 'type' and the default transport value 'streamable-http' are inline string literals (AGENTS.md §9: no hardcoded magic strings). 'streamable-http' is a protocol-defined canonical transport already named as a constant elsewhere (mcp_server_io/dialect.py CANONICAL_TRANSPORTS), and 'type' duplicates CURSOR_MCP_DIALECT.transport_fields[0]. Neither is named here, so the Cursor-specific default could silently drift from the shared dialect definition. Low blast radius (one function), but the duplication of an already-named protocol constant is the textbook drift hazard §9 warns about.

findings.evidence:
per_tool_only = dict(canonical.get("per_agentic_tool_only") or {})
    per_tool_only["cursor"] = ...
    canonical["per_agentic_tool_only"] = per_tool_only
    per_tool_extra = dict(canonical.get("per_agentic_tool_extra") or {})
    per_tool_extra["cursor"] = unknown_metadata_fields(frontmatter, ...)
    ...
    if "pair_id" in frontmatter:
        canonical["pair_id"] = str(frontmatter["pair_id"])
    elif prior_canonical is None:
        canonical["pair_id"] = new_pair_id()
findings.problem: The per_agentic_tool_only / per_agentic_tool_extra assignment block and the pair_id-fallback block (if 'pair_id' in frontmatter ... elif prior_canonical is None: new_pair_id()) are repeated near-verbatim across parse_cursor_agent_md, parse_cursor_skill_md, and parse_cursor_command_md. This is at the Rule-of-Three boundary (AGENTS.md §3). The blocks differ in their field-set arguments (CURSOR_AGENT_FIELDS vs CURSOR_SKILL_FIELDS vs empty), which is why they were not yet extracted; the pattern is shared across sibling adapters too, suggesting a candidate shared helper. Not a defect today — flagged as a duplication watch item.

findings.evidence: def parse_cursor_agent_md(text, prior_canonical=None, *, artifact_path: Path | None = None, artifact_root: Path | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
findings.problem: The (artifact_path, artifact_root) keyword pair co-occurs across all four parse signatures, and parse_cursor_agent_md / parse_cursor_rule_mdc discard artifact_root via 'del artifact_root' (line 97, 245) — they accept it only to satisfy a uniform signature. This is a mild data clump, but it is dictated by the CustomizationTypeIO port contract (architecture §5.1), which requires a uniform parse signature across all adapters; the unused-parameter 'del' is the idiomatic way to honor the contract without a linter warning. Acceptable given the port constraint; noted for completeness.

verdict_summary: Clean Layer-3 Cursor adapter; secret_policy threaded at both MCP egress boundaries; only minor notes (399-line soft-cap overage, a magic-string transport default, mild duplication/data-clump dictated by the port contract).
